classification.metric_note: Test-set metrics. Dummy always predicts 'no churn' (majority class).
classification.winner_reason: Selected on the validation set (ROC-AUC 0.862 vs XGBoost 0.859), confirmed on the untouched test set (0.837 vs 0.832) — a 0.005 gap on ~1,400 test rows is not statistically meaningful, so this is an effective tie. We prefer the simpler, more interpretable linear model given that tie.

regression.metric_note: Test-set metrics. Dummy always predicts the mean MonthlyCharges.
regression.winner_reason: Selected on the validation set (R2 0.9989 vs XGBoost 0.9988) — an effective tie, so we prefer the simpler, fully interpretable linear model. Verified the high R2 is not leakage or overfitting via four checks: train/val/test scores are nearly identical, 5-fold CV is stable, a permutation test on the target collapses R2 to ~0.03, and Ridge's coefficients recover a sensible, auditable rate card (within an identical service plan, MonthlyCharges varies by only ~$1 on average — billing is close to a deterministic function of plan features).
